title: The Sonnets CXXIII - No, Time, thou shalt not boast that I do change
content:
No, Time, thou shalt not boast that I do change:
    Thy pyramids built up with newer might
    To me are nothing novel, nothing strange;
    They are but dressings of a former sight.
    Our dates are brief, and therefore we admire
    What thou dost foist upon us that is old;
    And rather make them born to our desire
    Than think that we before have heard them told.
    Thy registers and thee I both defy,
    Not wondering at the present nor the past,
    For thy records and what we see doth lie,
    Made more or less by thy continual haste.
    This I do vow and this shall ever be;
    I will be true despite thy scythe and thee.
meaning:
William Shakespeare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Sonnets CXXIII - No, Time, thou shalt not boast that I do change"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
